meta.title: Cocora Valley | Quindío's Natural Landscape, Colombia
meta.description: Explore Cocora Valley, a natural reserve in Quindío, Colombia, featuring the world's tallest wax palms, cloud forests, and diverse Andean ecosystems.

hero.title: Cocora Valley
hero.subtitle: Colombia's Green Gem, Home of the Tallest Palm Trees
hero.hours: Nature Reserve / Cloud Forest / High-Altitude Hiking

intro.title: Explore Cocora Valley
intro.description:
Cocora Valley (Valle de Cocora) is a natural treasure in Quindío, Colombia, located about a 25-30 minute drive north of Salento. It is famous for the world's tallest palm trees—Colombia's national tree, the Wax Palm (Ceroxylon quindiuense), which averages 50-60 meters in height.

Serving as a buffer zone for Los Nevados National Natural Park, Cocora Valley offers breathtaking cloud forest landscapes, highly valuable scientific ecosystems, and multiple high-altitude hiking trails. It is an excellent place to observe endemic Andean flora and fauna, such as hummingbirds and Andean Condors, making it a non-profit natural landscape with immense ecological and educational value.

visitGuide.title: Preparation & Ecology
items: 🚙 Transport: Take a traditional WWII-era Willys Jeep from the main square (Plaza de Bolívar) in Salento. The ride takes about 25-30 minutes to the valley entrance. The first jeeps usually depart around 6:00 AM.

🥾 Extreme Mud Warning: Due to year-round rainfall, the trails are often extremely muddy and slippery. It is highly recommended to rent specialized rubber mud boots at the valley entrance or in Salento.
�️ Weather Alert: Regardless of the season, there is a high probability of fog and rain after midday. It is essential to start early and bring rain gear.

alsoKnownAs.title: Flora & Fauna
items: 🌴 Wax Palm (Ceroxylon quindiuense): Colombia's national tree, averaging 50-60 meters tall. Note that it is currently endangered as cattle grazing prevents the natural regeneration of seedlings.
� Andean Hummingbirds: At the Acaime hummingbird sanctuary, visitors can observe various wild hummingbirds endemic to the Andes up close.
🦅 Andean Condors: One of the largest flying birds in the world, occasionally spotted in the high-altitude areas of the valley.
♻️ Leave No Trace: Please stay on designated trails, do not leave trash behind, and help protect this fragile alpine ecosystem.

route.title: Trail Guide
route.overview: There are two main ways to explore Cocora Valley. Please choose based on your fitness level and time:
steps: 1. The Full Loop (Highly recommended counter-clockwise): Approx. 12-15 km | 5-6 hours | 600-800m elevation gain.

Highlights: Starting from the Blue Gate on the right of the entrance, you will first traverse the dense cloud forest, crossing five or six suspension bridges over rapid rivers. Midway, you can detour to the Acaime hummingbird sanctuary (extra fee required).
This is followed by a steep climb to the highest point, Finca La Montaña, before descending to the spectacular Wax Palm forest (Bosque de Palmas) for a perfect finish.
2. The Short Route (Clockwise): Approx. 2-3 km | 1.5-2 hours.

Highlights: If you are short on time or just want to see the tallest palm trees, you can head straight to the left main road from the entrance. This is a steep shortcut directly to Viewpoints 1 and 2, offering excellent morning light perfect for photography.
supplements: Entrance fees are charged in segments along the trail (no all-inclusive pass). The total cost is approximately COP $30,000 - $50,000. Please bring enough cash.
Entering the main palm forest, Finca El Portón, and the hummingbird sanctuary all require separate payments to private landowners.
The valley's altitude ranges from approximately 2,400 to 2,860 meters. Please be mindful of the physical exertion required at high altitudes.

sections.title: Ecological Status & Conservation
sections.content: The Wax Palm is an extremely precious and slow-growing plant. However, due to the expansion of local cattle farming, cows eat the young seedlings, causing the natural regeneration of the palm forest to stall, putting it in an endangered state. Protecting this ecosystem is urgent.

sections.title: Acaime Hummingbird Sanctuary
sections.content: As one of the most important nature educational stops on the hike, the Acaime sanctuary allows visitors to enjoy traditional 'hot chocolate with cheese' while observing wild hummingbirds endemic to the Andes.

sections.title: Trail Strategy: Clockwise vs. Counter-Clockwise
sections.content: Professional hikers recommend the counter-clockwise full loop: traversing the jungle and rivers first, tackling the steep climb, and saving the spectacular wax palm forest for the descent, which is also gentler on the knees. Those with limited physical stamina can opt for the clockwise short route directly to the viewpoints.

basicInfo.title: Quick Facts
basicInfo.typeValue: Nature Reserve / Cloud Forest / High-Altitude Hiking
basicInfo.cityValue: Wax Palm (Ceroxylon quindiuense), Andean Hummingbirds, Andean Condors
basicInfo.addressValue: Vía al Valle de Cocora, Km 11, Salento, Quindío, Colombia
basicInfo.petFriendlyValue: Pets allowed (Must be strictly leashed to protect wildlife)
basicInfo.ticketsValue: Segmented fees. Total approx. COP $30,000 - $50,000 (Bring cash)

hours.title: Opening Hours & Visitor Info
hours.summerTime: Excellent light and high chance to avoid afternoon fog and rain
hours.winterTime: Counter-clockwise Full Loop, Acaime Sanctuary, Wax Palm Forest, Suspension Bridges
hours.warningTime: The valley's weather changes rapidly, with a very high probability of fog and rain after midday, resulting in extremely muddy trails. It is strongly recommended to rent rubber mud boots and bring rain gear!
hours.tip: Hiking the full loop counter-clockwise saves the spectacular wax palm forest for the descent and effectively reduces pressure on the knees.

tickets.title: Important Information
tickets.outdoorPrice: Total approx. COP $30,000 - $50,000 (Paid in cash segments)
tickets.adultsPrice: Willys Jeep from Plaza de Bolívar, approx. 25-30 mins
tickets.cardPrice: Salento town, Acaime Sanctuary. 👉 [Go to Cocora Valley]
tickets.officialNotice: Much of the land here is privately owned, and hikers must pay cash separately at various private property checkpoints along the way. For more details, visit
tickets.officialNoticeLinkLabel: Official Website of Colombia's National Natural Parks

transport.title: Transportation
transport.airportDesc: From the nearest airports (Pereira or Armenia), you need to first reach Salento town, then take a Willys Jeep to Cocora Valley.
transport.selfDriveDesc: From Salento, follow the road for about 11 kilometers to reach the valley entrance. Road conditions may vary depending on the weather, so drive safely.
transport.busDesc: Take a traditional Willys Jeep from the main square (Plaza de Bolívar) in the center of Salento. The ride takes about 25-30 minutes.
transport.intercityDesc: The classic Full Loop is about 12-15 km long with an elevation gain of 600-800 meters. Trails are often muddy; renting rubber mud boots is recommended.
transport.cyclingDesc: Along the route, you will cross several wooden suspension bridges. Please follow local instructions and mind your safety.
transport.tipsDesc: The first Willys Jeeps usually depart around 6:00 AM. Start early to avoid afternoon rain and crowds.

gallery.subtitle: Feel Colombia's Most Magnificent Natural Landscape

reviews.title: Visitor Reviews
reviews.declaration: Review information can be viewed via Google Maps (external link).
reviews.moreReviews: View More Reviews on Google Maps

items.name: Carlos Mendoza
items.text: One of the most beautiful hiking spots in the world! The Wax Palms tower into the clouds, the scenery is breathtaking.

items.name: Li Xiaoming
items.text: Taking the Willys Jeep from Salento to get here is so much fun. The hiking trails are clear, the scenery is stunning, highly recommended!

items.name: María González
items.text: A must-visit place in Colombia! The morning mist makes the entire valley look like a fairyland.

items.name: John Smith
items.text: An unforgettable experience! I chose horseback riding, and the local guides were very friendly and professional.

items.name: Ana Silva
items.text: Beautiful scenery, but it gets quite crowded on weekends. Recommend visiting on weekdays for a better experience in nature.

items.name: Zhang Wei
items.text: Absolutely worth a visit! Remember to bring your camera, every angle is a postcard-perfect view.

items.name: Roberto Pérez
items.text: This is Colombia's pride! The Wax Palm is the national tree, and seeing them so tall is truly awe-inspiring.

items.name: Emma Wilson
items.text: I completed the full hiking trail, which took about 6 hours. Tiring but absolutely worth it!

historyTimeline.title: Historical Timeline

items.title: Early Period: Nature Conservation & Discovery
items.description: Cocora Valley, as part of Los Nevados National Natural Park, has long been an important nature reserve and biodiversity hotspot in Colombia.

items.title: 20th Century: Eco-Tourism Development
items.description: With the growth of tourism in Colombia, Cocora Valley gradually became one of the most popular eco-tourism destinations, attracting visitors from around the world.

items.title: 21st Century: World Heritage Recognition
items.description: As part of the Colombian Coffee Cultural Landscape, the region was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List, gaining international recognition and protection.

historyTimeline.guideContent: When visiting, you can take a Willys Jeep from Salento to Cocora Valley; then follow the hiking trails to admire the Wax Palm forest and cloud forest; if you have enough time, you can hike or horseback ride in the valley to experience Colombia's natural beauty. Finally, return to Salento to explore the charm of the town.

officialManagement.title: About Cocora Valley
officialManagement.text: Cocora Valley is a famous natural landscape in Quindío, Colombia, known for the world's tallest palm trees—the Wax Palm. It is managed by Colombia's National Natural Parks administration and relevant local governments.
